gotchas: Missing Many2one root in @api.depends path — use full dotted path 'fiscal_position_id' AND 'fiscal_position_id.country_id'
Don't add @api.depends on related= field — Odoo auto-declares dependency chain
Cross-company field needs additional @api.depends_context('company') if multi-company aware
Missing @api.depends_context('lang') makes computed translatable fields cache-leak across users
Order matters: @api.depends_context BEFORE @api.depends — both decorators stack
Field must have translate=True if the compute uses translatable inputs
Don't use @api.model + def create(self, vals) — silently broken on batch import in v17+
super().create(vals_list) takes the full list, not single dict in loop
@api.model_create_single is removed in v18 — defaults to multi everywhere
Reading old values AFTER super().write() returns the new value — read BEFORE
Always return the result of super().write() — Odoo expects bool
Iterating self after write may trigger recompute; use rec.id snapshot to avoid

snippet_text:
<record id="view_sale_order_form_inherit" model="ir.ui.view">
    <field name="inherit_id" ref="sale.view_order_form"/>
    <field name="arch" type="xml">
        <xpath expr="//field[@name='partner_id']" position="after">
            <field name="x_custom_field"/>
        </xpath>
    </xpath>
</record>
gotchas: position="replace" wipes content + breaks downstream extension modules — prefer 'inside'/'after'/'before'/'attributes'
If you MUST replace, replace exactly the smallest target node — never replace whole <form>
Replacing a <field> drops its widget options + invisible attrs from base — re-declare them all

snippet_text:
<!-- BAD: matches any <button> in form, ambiguous -->
<xpath expr="//button" position="before">...</xpath>

<!-- GOOD: anchors on name attribute, deterministic -->
<xpath expr="//button[@name='action_confirm']" position="before">
    <button name="action_my_check" type="object" string="Check"/>
</xpath>
gotchas: Generic xpath like //field or //button matches first occurrence — module load order changes break it
Always anchor on stable attribute (name/id) or pair (model+name)
Avoid xpath by index //field[3] — count drifts when other modules insert siblings
Class.include() is removed in v17 — use patch(Class.prototype, {...})
Always call super.method() inside the patched method — patch doesn't auto-chain
Patch second arg in v17+ is plain object literal (no { name: ... } wrapper from older signature)
_inherits is DELEGATION (composes a different model via FK) — _inherit is EXTENSION (same model)
_inherits via_field MUST be required=True + ondelete='cascade' — Odoo expects ownership
_inherits delegates field reads + writes; _inherit shares the table
Action methods MUST return the result of super() — caller may expect dict (action_window) or bool
Never return None silently — clients raise 'Action returned no value'
Side effects after super() — never replace super() with manual workflow logic
create/write/unlink/copy MUST return — super() result preserves recordset/bool contract
Never `super().write(vals); return self` — write returns bool, not self
unlink that returns False without raising silently aborts deletion

snippet_text:
# ANTI-PATTERN — Python 2 idiom, useless boilerplate in Python 3:
res = super(SaleOrder, self.with_context(...)).action_confirm()

# CORRECT — Python 3 super():
res = super().action_confirm()
gotchas: Python 3 super() auto-binds the class — explicit ClassName argument is redundant
Exception: super(ClassName, self.with_context(...)) IS still valid when self is rebound
Mixed style across class methods is hard to read — pick one and stick to it

snippet_text:
# ANTI-PATTERN — caller gets None, breaks chain:
def action_post(self):
    super().action_post()
    self._do_extra()

# CORRECT — preserve super() return for downstream callers:
def action_post(self):
    res = super().action_post()
    self._do_extra()
    return res
gotchas: Forgetting return after super() is the #1 silent override bug — chain breaks at the first hop
Linter pylint-odoo: W7901 method-required-super (V18+: super-with-arguments)
Test pattern: assert res is not None — None means override forgot return
Computed fields used in domain/groupby/search MUST have store=True — else slow N+1 query
store=True requires explicit @api.depends — partial deps cause stale data
Add `index=True` if field is in WHERE clause of frequent queries (audit slow_query log first)
Treat vals_list as ALL records being created in one call — single-record assumption causes O(N²) slowness
Don't `for vals in vals_list: super().create([vals])` — defeats batching
If you need per-record post-processing, do it AFTER super() returns the created recordset
Each Many2one hop in the path needs its own @api.depends entry — not just the final field
Missing intermediate field = no recompute when partner_id is REASSIGNED
Lambda in compute body that walks .partner_id.country_id is fine; depends still needs full chain

snippet_text:
# v17 (deprecated):
qty_invoiced = fields.Float(group_operator='sum')

# v18+:
qty_invoiced = fields.Float(aggregator='sum')
gotchas: field option group_operator renamed to aggregator in v18 — silent ignore in v18 if you keep old name
Lint rule W8104 flags group_operator usage on v18 codebase
Migration script: regex replace `group_operator=` → `aggregator=` per-version safe
name_get() removed entirely in v18 — must override _compute_display_name with @api.depends
display_name field is auto-declared by Odoo; you only override the compute
Calling self.name_get() in v18 raises AttributeError; calling in v17 returns [(id, label)] list

snippet_text:
<!-- ANTI-PATTERN (removed v17): -->
<field name="partner_id" states="draft,sent"/>

<!-- CORRECT v17+ — direct attrs in XML view: -->
<field name="partner_id" readonly="state not in ['draft','sent']"/>
gotchas: field `states=...` attribute removed v17 — use `readonly="<expr>"` / `invisible="<expr>"` directly
Old `attrs="{'invisible': [('state', '!=', 'draft')]}"` also removed v17 — same direct-expression migration
Lint rule W8201 catches old states= on v17+; use ruff custom rule or grep -rn 'states='

snippet_text:
# v17/v18:
from odoo.tools import safe_eval
result = safe_eval(expr, locals_dict)

# v19 (signature changed — explicit parameter names):
from odoo.tools.safe_eval import safe_eval
result = safe_eval(expr, locals_dict=locals_dict, mode='eval')
gotchas: v19 makes locals_dict a keyword-only argument — positional call breaks
Import path stable: from odoo.tools.safe_eval import safe_eval (the module-level shortcut still works)
mode='eval' is the default; pass 'exec' for statement evaluation (rare in user code)

snippet_text:
# v17/v18:
groups = self.env['sale.order'].read_group(
    [('state', '=', 'sale')], ['amount_total:sum'], ['partner_id'])

# v19+:
groups = self.env['sale.order']._read_group(
    [('state', '=', 'sale')], groupby=['partner_id'],
    aggregates=['amount_total:sum'])
gotchas: read_group is now an internal-only API in v19 — public callers should use _read_group / formatted_read_group
_read_group returns rows as list of dicts (same shape) but kwarg names changed: aggregates= replaces fields=
formatted_read_group adds rendering metadata for kanban/dashboard — pick by use case
v17+ post_init signature is post_init(env) — old (cr, registry) is removed
Use parameterized %s + tuple — never f-string the value into SQL (SQL injection)
self.env.cr.execute() does NOT auto-flush — call self.env.flush_all() if reading data just written via ORM

snippet_text:
class SaleOrder(models.Model):
    _name = 'sale.order'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']
    _description = 'Sales Order'

    state = fields.Selection([...], tracking=True)
gotchas: Add 'mail.thread' to _inherit list — never derive from mail.thread base class
Fields with tracking=True automatically post audit messages on write — costs DB write per change
Don't override message_post unless you know the chatter format contract — use sub_type_xmlid arg instead
activity_schedule needs the activity_type xmlid — never look up by name, breaks on translation
user_id arg defaults to current user — pass explicit user_id for delegated activities
date_deadline accepts date or relative offset; datetime gets truncated to date silently
After raw SQL UPDATE that bypasses ORM, call invalidate_recordset to drop cache
modified() triggers recompute lazily on next read — explicit recompute() rarely needed
v17 dropped self.env['x'].recompute() — replaced by env.flush_all() + invalidate_*
Self-referencing @api.depends('parent_id.X') needs `recursive=True` ONLY on field definition (not decorator)
Recursive compute can hit recursion limit — keep tree depth bounded or batch process
Don't recompute on write of every child — use store=True + index for parent_path

snippet_text:
currency_id = fields.Many2one('res.currency', related='company_id.currency_id', store=True)
amount_total = fields.Monetary(
    string='Total',
    compute='_compute_amounts',
    store=True,
    currency_field='currency_id',
)
gotchas: fields.Monetary REQUIRES a currency_field reference — silently uses 'currency_id' if omitted
Without currency_field, multi-currency rounding is wrong (uses company default, not record's currency)
Always store the related currency_id with store=True — domain queries on currency need it indexed
selection_add appends — never replace `selection=[...]` to extend
ondelete is MANDATORY when selection_add is used — controls behavior when key disabled
Don't forget to also add the value to dependent compute fields' state-handling

snippet_text:
tag_ids = fields.Many2many(
    'account.account.tag',
    'account_account_account_tag',  # explicit junction table name
    'account_id', 'tag_id',
    string='Tags',
)
gotchas: Explicit relation table name avoids auto-generated 60-char-truncated default name
PostgreSQL identifier max 63 chars — auto-name like rel_account_account_account_account_tag_rel may collide
When 2 modules add Many2many between same models, explicit table prevents ambiguity

snippet_text:
domain = [
    ('state', 'in', ('sale', 'done')),
    ('partner_id.country_id.code', '=', 'VN'),
    '|',
    ('amount_total', '>', 1000),
    ('user_id', '=', self.env.user.id),
]
gotchas: Domain leaf MUST be 3-element tuple/list: (field, operator, value)
Logical operators '|' '&' '!' are PREFIX (Polish notation) — they apply to the NEXT N leaves, not infix
Dotted-path leaves like 'partner_id.country_id.code' work for read but slow under search — store=True the chain

snippet_text:
# Don't mutate self.env.context — it's frozen.
new_ctx = dict(self.env.context, lang='vi_VN', tracking_disable=True)
records = self.env['res.partner'].with_context(new_ctx).create(vals_list)

# Or shorthand kwargs:
records = self.env['res.partner'].with_context(lang='vi_VN').create(vals_list)
gotchas: self.env.context is frozendict in v17+ — direct mutation raises TypeError
with_context kwargs ADD to existing ctx; pass dict to REPLACE (rarely useful)
tracking_disable=True suppresses mail.thread audit — useful for bulk create perf

snippet_text:
# Operate as a specific company — sets context['allowed_company_ids']
records = self.env['account.journal'].with_company(target_company).search(
    [('type', '=', 'sale')]
)

# Wrong: only changing company_id field doesn't fix record_rules
# rec.company_id = target_company  # rules still filter by env.company
gotchas: with_company resets env.company AND allowed_company_ids — affects record_rules domain
Don't pass company_id field assignment to switch — record rules still use env.company
Multi-company create: with_company(C).create(vals) sets correct default company_id

snippet_text:
# sudo() — bypass record rules + ACL, run as superuser
orders = self.env['sale.order'].sudo().search([('partner_id', '=', portal_partner.id)])

# with_user() — switch effective user (still subject to that user's rights)
records = self.env['res.partner'].with_user(target_user).search([])
gotchas: sudo() is a security knife — escapes record rules; only use when you've validated the access yourself
with_user is for AS-IF impersonation — record rules still apply per that user's groups
Audit log: sudo() bypass is invisible in CRUD log — log explicitly when sudo() critical actions

snippet_text:
<record id="sale_order_personal_rule" model="ir.rule">
    <field name="name">Personal Sale Orders</field>
    <field name="model_id" ref="model_sale_order"/>
    <field name="domain_force">[('user_id', '=', user.id)]</field>
    <field name="groups" eval="[(4, ref('sales_team.group_sale_salesman'))]"/>
</record>
gotchas: domain_force is a Python-string expression evaluated PER REQUEST — keep it cheap (no heavy compute)
User context: `user`, `user.id`, `time`, `datetime`, `relativedelta` available; no other ORM calls
Empty groups => global rule applied to ALL users (rare); always scope groups for personal/team rules

snippet_text:
# v17/v18:
_sql_constraints = [
    ('name_uniq', 'UNIQUE(name, company_id)', 'Reference must be unique per company'),
]

# v19+ class-level Constraint:
_constraints = [
    models.Constraint('UNIQUE(name, company_id)', 'Reference must be unique per company'),
]
gotchas: v19 introduces models.Constraint replacing _sql_constraints — both supported in transition window
Constraint name must be globally unique across the DB — namespace with module prefix
Composite UNIQUE on (name, company_id) — multi-company friendly; UNIQUE(name) breaks multi-company

snippet_text:
# UNSAFE — SQL injection vector:
# self.env.cr.execute(f"SELECT * FROM res_partner WHERE name = '{name}'")

# SAFE — parameterized %s + tuple:
self.env.cr.execute(
    "SELECT id FROM res_partner WHERE name = %s AND active = %s",
    (name, True),
)
gotchas: NEVER f-string user-controlled values into SQL — even % old-style format is unsafe
Use psycopg2 %s placeholder + tuple of params — psycopg2 escapes types correctly
For dynamic identifiers (column names), use psycopg2.sql.Identifier — never string-concat

snippet_text:
# v17+: search_fetch combines search + read in one DB roundtrip
orders = self.env['sale.order'].search_fetch(
    [('state', '=', 'sale')],
    ['name', 'partner_id', 'amount_total'],
)
for order in orders:
    # No additional SELECT — fields already prefetched
    print(order.name, order.partner_id.name)
gotchas: search_fetch eagerly loads given fields — saves N+1 vs search()+read() pattern
Don't pass relational fields directly: pass m2o name first, then `.partner_id.name` triggers ONE batch SELECT
v16- doesn't have search_fetch — use search().read([fields]) but accept slight overhead

snippet_text:
# Foreign-key delete behavior — choose deliberately:
order_id = fields.Many2one('sale.order', ondelete='cascade', required=True)
product_id = fields.Many2one('product.product', ondelete='restrict')
salesman_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', ondelete='set null')
gotchas: Default ondelete='set null' — silent data loss on parent delete unless required=True
ondelete='cascade' is irreversible at DB level — use sparingly + only for child-of-aggregate rels
ondelete='restrict' forces user to delete children first — better default for shared masters

snippet_text:
<!-- v17 and earlier: -->
<tree string="Sales Orders">
    <field name="name"/>
</tree>

<!-- v18+: -->
<list string="Sales Orders">
    <field name="name"/>
</list>
gotchas: <tree> tag renamed to <list> in v18 — silently ignored by v18 parser if you keep <tree>
view_type='list' field on ir.ui.view also valid in v18; v17 still says 'tree'
Migrate XML: sed -i 's/<tree/<list/g; s/<\/tree>/<\/list>/g' on the xml dir

snippet_text:
<!-- v16 and earlier (deprecated): -->
<span t-esc="order.amount_total"/>

<!-- v17+: -->
<span t-out="order.amount_total"/>
gotchas: t-esc renamed to t-out in v17 — t-esc still works as alias but flagged by ESLint odoo rule
t-raw removed in v17 — use t-out + safe filter explicitly if you need unescaped HTML
t-out auto-escapes; pass t-options='{'widget': 'monetary'}' for currency-aware rendering
@api.constrains validates AFTER write/create — raises ValidationError to roll back
Use ValidationError (user-facing) — never raise UserError or generic Exception
Pass tuple of field names — wildcards/dotted-paths NOT supported (compute on-the-fly inside method)
@api.onchange runs ONLY in form view — NOT during ORM create/write/import — data inconsistency risk
Prefer @api.depends compute fields — applies everywhere (UI, API, scheduled actions, import)
Use onchange for UX hints (warning dialog, dynamic domain) — use compute for invariants
Guard the transition first (state check) — never assume button-click guarantees correct state
Bulk write (single self.write) is faster than per-record loop for state changes
Return True (or action_window dict) — UI uses return value to refresh / open next view

snippet_text:
<list decoration-success="state == 'sale'"
      decoration-warning="state == 'sent'"
      decoration-muted="state == 'cancel'">
    <field name="name"/>
    <field name="state"/>
</list>
gotchas: decoration-* expression uses CURRENT record's fields — `state == 'sale'` not array form
Available classes: success/warning/danger/muted/info/primary — NO custom CSS hex codes
v18: tag is <list>, attribute name unchanged. v17 and below: <tree decoration-*>

snippet_text:
<!-- PRIMARY view (mode='primary' implicit when no inherit_id): -->
<record id="view_order_form" model="ir.ui.view">
    <field name="model">sale.order</field>
    <field name="arch" type="xml">
        <form>...</form>
    </field>
</record>

<!-- EXTENSION view: -->
<record id="view_order_form_inherit" model="ir.ui.view">
    <field name="inherit_id" ref="sale.view_order_form"/>
    <field name="arch" type="xml">
        <xpath ...>...</xpath>
    </field>
</record>
gotchas: Don't mix <form> root + <xpath> children in same arch — extension views start from <xpath> ONLY
mode='primary' (default for inherit_id=null) replaces the view; mode='extension' merges into base
ref= path: 'module.xmlid' — same module can use just 'xmlid' but be explicit for cross-module refs
@api.private decorator added in v19 — marks method as not exposed over web RPC
Underscore-prefix _name is convention but NOT enforced by RPC layer; @api.private is hard guard
Only ORM models use @api.private effectively — OWL component methods don't need it

snippet_text:
name = fields.Char(string='Reference', required=True, translate=False)
description = fields.Html('Description', translate=True)
# translate=True stores translations in jsonb column (v17+) or ir.translation (v16-)
gotchas: translate=True is forever — once set, downgrading translate=False loses all translations
v17+: translations stored as jsonb in same column; v16-: separate ir.translation rows
Don't put translate=True on Char that holds technical reference (codes, IDs) — bloats DB
Callable selection is recomputed PER CALL — cache result if expensive
selection=fn must accept self — recordset method, not @api.model
Combining selection_add= with callable parent base does NOT work — pick one

snippet_text:
credit_limit = fields.Float(
    string='Credit Limit',
    company_dependent=True,
    digits='Account',
    groups='account.group_account_user',
)
gotchas: company_dependent=True stores per-company values via ir.property — same record can have N values
Reads in env.company context only — switch via with_company() for cross-company analysis
Don't mix company_dependent with required=True — Odoo raises (no global default possible)

snippet_text:
amount_residual = fields.Monetary(
    string='Amount Due',
    compute='_compute_amount',
    store=True,
    groups='account.group_account_invoice,account.group_account_readonly',
)
gotchas: groups= attribute hides field at FIELD LEVEL — record rules still apply on top
Comma-separated xmlids — ANY group satisfies (OR). For AND, use multiple field declarations
Hidden field not transmitted to client — won't appear in form view even without xpath modification

snippet_text:
import logging
_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Inside method:
_logger.info('Order %s confirmed by user %s', order.name, user.login)
_logger.warning('Slow query took %.2fs', elapsed)
gotchas: Never `print()` in addons — bypasses Odoo log level filters + JSON log format
Use lazy %s formatting — _logger.info('%s', x) NOT _logger.info(f'{x}') (perf when log level filtered)
Log level: debug/info/warning/error/critical — match severity to operational need

snippet_text:
user_id = fields.Many2one(
    'res.users',
    default=lambda self: self.env.user,
)
date_order = fields.Datetime(default=fields.Datetime.now)
company_id = fields.Many2one(
    'res.company',
    default=lambda self: self.env.company,
    required=True,
)
gotchas: Callable default receives `self` — use lambda or method reference, NOT bare call like `self.env.user`
fields.Datetime.now is class-method ref (no parens) — `now()` would evaluate at module import
Default lambdas are evaluated PER create call — no caching, no closure over outer scope

snippet_text:
<record id="ir_cron_mail_scheduler" model="ir.cron">
    <field name="name">Mail: Send/Receive</field>
    <field name="model_id" ref="model_mail_mail"/>
    <field name="state">code</field>
    <field name="code">model.process_email_queue()</field>
    <field name="interval_type">minutes</field>
    <field name="interval_number">1</field>
</record>
gotchas: Cron `code` runs in batch — wrap heavy work in self.env.cr.savepoint() to limit damage on partial failure
interval_type/number combined determines next-run; v17+ also accepts cron expressions in `nextcall`
Long-running cron blocks worker — split into chunks + commit periodically (cr.commit()) to avoid timeout
Props are READ-ONLY — never mutate this.props.X (silently ignored, breaks tests)
useState returns proxy — must wrap object literal in useState({...}); plain class field is NOT reactive
Computed values from props: use getter `get total() { return this.props.x * 2 }` — re-runs on prop change
useService MUST be called in setup() — hook order matters, never inside async fn or conditional
orm.searchRead replaces deprecated this.rpc(/web/dataset/search_read) — same payload
Don't import orm directly from a module — useService is required for proper teardown / mock

snippet_text:
IrAttachment = request.env['ir.attachment']

# Avoid using sudo when not necessary: internal users can create attachments,
# as opposed to public and portal users.
if not request.env.user._is_internal():
    IrAttachment = IrAttachment.sudo()

attachment = IrAttachment.create({
    'name': name,
    'datas': base64.b64encode(file.read()),
    'res_model': 'mail.compose.message',
    'res_id': 0,
})
gotchas: `request.env.user._is_internal()` returns False for portal AND public users — use `_is_portal()` separately if you need to distinguish them; confusing the two can grant public users portal-level access.
Applying `sudo()` to the full `request.env` (not just the model recordset) widens privileges for every subsequent ORM call in the same scope; always narrow it to a single model as shown.
`request.env` is bound to the current HTTP user — do NOT cache a sudo'd env between requests; a shared module-level variable retains elevated rights across sessions.

`inherit_id="portal.portal_breadcrumbs"` extends the breadcrumb list; `inherit_id="portal.portal_layout"` wraps the whole page — using the wrong one results in duplicate navigation bars or missing layout wrappers.

`priority` on a `<template>` controls render order when multiple modules inherit the same template; lower number = rendered first; a missing priority defaults to 16 which can silently override community templates.
`t-attf-class` uses `#{}` interpolation (Jinja-style), NOT `${}` — mixing the syntaxes produces a literal `${}` string in the rendered HTML without any error.

snippet_text:
def _portal_ensure_token(self):
    """ Get the current record access token """
    if not self.access_token:
        # we use a `write` to force the cache clearing otherwise
        # `return self.access_token` will return False
        self.sudo().write({'access_token': str(uuid.uuid4())})
    return self.access_token
gotchas: `self.sudo().write(...)` is required (not just field assignment) because Odoo's ORM field cache is NOT invalidated by a plain `self.access_token = ...` assignment on a stored field — the stale cached value would be returned.
`portal.mixin` adds `access_token` but does NOT add `access_url` logic for your model — you must override `_compute_access_url` to return a meaningful URL otherwise `access_url` is always `'#'`.

Never expose `access_token` in a context key or URL fragment that is logged by a proxy/firewall; treat it as a secret credential — even a Read-only portal share token grants full record read access to anyone who has it.

snippet_text:
countries = request.env['res.country'].sudo().search([])
states = request.env['res.country.state'].sudo().search([])

values = {
    'partner': partner,
    'countries': countries,
    'states': states,
    'has_check_vat': hasattr(request.env['res.partner'], 'check_vat'),
}
gotchas: Portal users typically have no read rights on `res.country` by default — `sudo()` is needed for multi-country selector widgets; omitting it raises `AccessError` silently handled as a 500 in production.

`.sudo().search([])` fetches ALL records regardless of `ir.rule` domain restrictions — if the model has multi-company rules, always add an explicit company filter: `.sudo().search([('company_id', '=', ...)])` or `('company_id', 'in', request.env.companies.ids)`.

`request.env['res.partner']` used bare (without sudo) still reflects the portal user's rights — `hasattr` for optional module features like `check_vat` is safe because it is a Python attribute check, not an ORM query.

`default_get` receives only the list of field names declared in the view — if a field is not in the view it will NOT be in the `fields` arg; always guard with `if 'my_field' in fields` before setting it to avoid silently ignored defaults.

Always call `super().default_get(fields)` first and update the returned dict — skipping super loses defaults set by parent classes (e.g. `_defaults` class attribute or `default=` on field definition).

`self.env.context.get('active_ids')` can be a list of IDs from multi-select; `active_id` is only the last-clicked record in a list view and may differ from `active_ids[0]` when records are selected in reverse order — always prefer `active_ids` for batch wizards.

Returning `{'type': 'ir.actions.act_window_close'}` closes the dialog and reloads the underlying list/form; returning `None` or omitting a return also closes the dialog but does NOT reload — use `act_window_close` when changes must be reflected immediately.

To re-open the current wizard (multi-step): return a new `ir.actions.act_window` dict pointing to the same `res_model`; the previous wizard record stays in the DB until the `ir.autovacuum` cron cleans stale `TransientModel` rows.

Returning an `ir.actions.act_window` dict that opens another model's form view (e.g. to show the created invoice) requires setting both `res_model` AND `res_id` for a single-record form view; omitting `res_id` opens a new empty form.
`default_get` must return One2many/Many2many values as ORM command tuples `(0, 0, vals)` for create — NOT as plain dicts; the webclient deserialises the return value differently from `create()`.

The comment `res['pick_ids'][0][2]` unpacks a `(6, 0, ids)` Command 6 tuple: index 0=command, 1=0 (unused), 2=list of ids; this pattern is brittle — use `odoo.fields.Command.set(ids)` in v16+ for clarity.

Setting an x2m field value in `default_get` creates those child records immediately on save — if the user cancels the wizard without saving, those records are never written; do not create records with side-effects inside `default_get`.

`self.with_company(company_id)` switches the active company context for sequence generation and fiscal-position lookup — omitting it causes the sequence to increment on the wrong company's counter when creating records via a background job.

`with_company` does NOT change `self.env.user` — it only updates `self.env.company` and `self.env.companies`; access rights are still evaluated for the original user, so privileged background jobs must also use `with_user(SUPERUSER_ID)` if needed.

Reassigning `self = self.with_company(...)` inside a loop replaces the local variable but does NOT mutate the original recordset for callers of this method; the pattern is valid only within the same method scope.

snippet_text:
<record id="sale_order_comp_rule" model="ir.rule">
    <field name="name">Sales Order multi-company</field>
    <field name="model_id" ref="model_sale_order"/>
    <field name="domain_force">[('company_id', 'in', company_ids)]</field>
</record>
gotchas: `company_ids` in `domain_force` is a special ORM-evaluated variable that resolves to the list of all company IDs the current user has access to — it is NOT a Python variable; using `env.company.id` instead would restrict to the single active company and break multi-company switching.

Omitting `<field name="groups">` makes the rule apply to ALL users including admin — always explicitly scope rules to specific groups or add `global="True"` only when truly universal restriction is intended.

When `perm_read`, `perm_write`, `perm_create`, `perm_unlink` are all omitted, the rule defaults to applying only to read; explicitly declare all four perms you intend to restrict to avoid silently missing write/unlink protection.

snippet_text:
property_account_payable_id = fields.Many2one(
    'account.account',
    company_dependent=True,
    string='Account Payable',
    domain="[('account_type', '=', 'liability_payable'),"
           " ('deprecated', '=', False)]",
    help='This account will be used instead of the default one'
         ' as the payable account for the current partner',
    required=True
)
gotchas: `company_dependent=True` stores values in `ir.property` keyed by `(field, res_id, company_id)` — `search()` on a company-dependent field is expensive (cross-joins `ir.property`) and should be avoided in large datasets; use a regular stored field with a multi-company default method instead when you need fast search.

The `default=` kwarg on a `company_dependent` field is ignored at runtime; Odoo replaces it with `_default_company_dependent()` which reads from `ir.property`; set per-company defaults via `ir.property` records in a data XML file instead.

Deleting a company in a multi-company setup does NOT cascade-delete its `ir.property` rows — stale property values for a deleted company survive and can silently re-appear if a new company is created with the same ID.

snippet_text:
attachment = IrAttachment.create({
    'name': name,
    'datas': base64.b64encode(file.read()),
    'res_model': 'mail.compose.message',
    'res_id': 0,
    'access_token': IrAttachment._generate_access_token(),
})
gotchas: Setting `res_model='mail.compose.message'` and `res_id=0` is the standard Odoo pattern for a 'pending' attachment that will be linked to the real record when the message is posted — do NOT set the final `res_model`/`res_id` before the record exists or the attachment becomes orphaned if the message creation fails.

`datas` expects a base64-encoded `bytes` string — passing raw bytes raises a validation error; always call `base64.b64encode(raw).decode()` (Python str) not just `base64.b64encode(raw)` (bytes) because the ORM validates string type.

Attachments with no `res_model`/`res_id` (or the staging `mail.compose.message` placeholder) are collected by `ir.autovacuum._gc_attachments()` if not linked within ~7 days — long-lived pending attachments must be linked to a real record or they will be deleted silently.

snippet_text:
invoice_pdf_report_file = fields.Binary(
    attachment=True,
    string='PDF File',
    copy=False,
)
gotchas: `attachment=True` stores the binary content in `ir.attachment` instead of the model's database column — the field value in the model table is `NULL`; querying with SQL directly will always return `NULL` for that column, confusing reports that bypass the ORM.

Copying a record with `copy=False` preserves the `attachment=True` semantic correctly; omitting `copy=False` copies the attachment record but creates a shared `ir.attachment` with the same `res_id` pointing to the original — mutating the copy's binary would silently corrupt the original.

`attachment=True` fields are lazily loaded — reading the field fetches from `ir.attachment` and populates the cache; accessing hundreds of records in a loop triggers N+1 attachment queries unless you `sudo().read(['invoice_pdf_report_file'])` in a single call first.

The `download=True` query parameter triggers `Content-Disposition: attachment` — omitting it serves the file inline (e.g. PDFs open in the browser tab instead of downloading); always add `?download=1` when building download buttons.

Public attachments (`ir.attachment.public=True`) are served without authentication; private attachments require either a valid session or an `access_token` query parameter — missing `access_token` for portal share links returns HTTP 403 with no descriptive error.

The route `/web/content/<int:id>` resolves `ir.attachment` by default; to serve a binary field from another model use the long form `/web/content/<model>/<id>/<field>` — using the short form for custom models returns `not found`.

`onMounted` fires after the first render when the DOM node is available — `this.ref.el` is `null` BEFORE `onMounted`; accessing `useRef(...).el` in `setup()` synchronously always returns `null` and causes silent failures.
Do NOT call `onMounted` inside an `if` branch or a loop — OWL hooks must be called unconditionally in the same `setup()` order on every render; conditional hook calls throw a runtime OWL error.
Use `onWillUnmount` to clean up DOM event listeners registered in `onMounted`; forgetting cleanup causes memory leaks for components that mount/unmount frequently (e.g. modal dialogs).

`useState` returns an OWL reactive Proxy — mutating a nested object inside the proxy (e.g. `this.state.nested.x = 1`) DOES trigger re-render only if `nested` was itself wrapped in `useState`; a plain nested object reference is not observed.

Replacing the entire state object (`this.state = { ... }`) silently breaks reactivity because you are reassigning the local variable, not mutating the proxy — always mutate existing proxy properties instead.

`useState(this.env.dialogData)` subscribes this component to the env-level reactive object — multiple components reading the same `useState`-wrapped object share state updates; changes in one component re-render ALL subscribers.

snippet_text: Tax 15%
gotchas: `t-attf-class` uses `{{ }}` for inline Python expressions (Mako-style) while `t-att-class` expects a pure expression string — mixing them (`t-att-class="'a ' + ..."`) is valid but `t-attf-class` is preferred for mixed literal+expression strings.
In QWeb XML, `<` must be escaped as `<` inside attribute values — `{{ len(x) < 10 }}` causes an XML parse error; always use `<` for comparison operators inside `t-attf-*` attributes.

`t-attf-class` APPENDS to any static `class="..."` attribute on the same element — if both are present the final class list is concatenated; using both together is valid but unintentional duplicates can create rendering issues.

`patch()` modifies the prototype in-place globally — patching a component used in multiple views will affect ALL instances; use `owl Component inheritance` (extend the class) instead when you only need to override behaviour in one context.

Always call `super.setup(...arguments)` inside a patched `setup()` — skipping it prevents the parent's hooks (`useService`, `useState`, etc.) from running, causing `undefined` service references or missing reactive state.

`patch` is idempotent only if you import and apply it once; applying the same patch twice (e.g. due to circular imports or repeated module loading in tests) stacks the patches and calls `super.setup()` twice.

snippet_text:
id,name,model_id:id,group_id:id,perm_read,perm_write,perm_create,perm_unlink
access_sale_order_salesman,sale_order salesman,sale.model_sale_order,sales_team.group_sale_salesman,1,0,0,0
access_sale_order_manager,sale_order manager,sale.model_sale_order,sales_team.group_sale_manager,1,1,1,1
gotchas: `model_id:id` must reference the model's XML ID in the format `<addon>.model_<model_name_with_underscores>` — using the model's technical name (`sale.order`) directly raises an `External ID not found` error during module install.

Omitting a row for a group means that group inherits access only from parent groups (via `implied_ids`) — if the parent group has no access either, the user gets AccessError; always add explicit rows for every group that needs access, even if it only inherits.

The CSV `id` column must be globally unique across all installed addons — duplicate IDs in different modules silently overwrite each other during install; prefix with the module name (e.g. `access_sale_order_manager`) to avoid collisions.

snippet_text:
<record id="sale_order_rule_portal" model="ir.rule">
    <field name="name">Portal Personal Quotations/Sales Orders</field>
    <field name="model_id" ref="sale.model_sale_order"/>
    <field name="domain_force">[('message_partner_ids','child_of',[user.commercial_partner_id.id])]</field>
    <field name="groups" eval="[(4, ref('base.group_portal'))]"/>
    <field name="perm_unlink" eval="True"/>
    <field name="perm_write" eval="True"/>
    <field name="perm_read" eval="True"/>
    <field name="perm_create" eval="False"/>
</record>
gotchas: `child_of` in `domain_force` evaluates against the partner hierarchy at query time — if a portal user's `commercial_partner_id` is not set (e.g. for a standalone contact without a company), the domain resolves to no records; always ensure portal users have a properly set `commercial_partner_id`.

`eval="[(4, ref('base.group_portal'))]"` adds the group to the rule's `groups` Many2many; using Command 6 `[(6, 0, [ref(...)])]` would REPLACE all groups — use command 4 (link) to safely append to existing groups.

Portal rules apply ON TOP OF model-level ACL — a portal user who passes the `ir.rule` filter but lacks `perm_read=1` in `ir.model.access.csv` still gets `AccessError`; both ACL and record rules must allow access.

snippet_text:
credit_limit = fields.Float(
    string='Credit Limit',
    help='Credit limit specific to this partner.',
    groups='account.group_account_invoice,account.group_account_readonly',
    company_dependent=True,
    copy=False,
    readonly=False
)
gotchas: The `groups=` attribute on a field definition restricts both VIEW visibility AND ORM read access — a user outside the listed groups who reads the field via `sudo()` will see the value, but the webclient will not render it in views; use `sudo()` intentionally only for backend logic, never to bypass field-level security in portal/website routes.
Multiple groups in `groups=` are comma-separated and evaluated as OR — the field is visible if the user belongs to ANY of the listed groups; there is no AND syntax for field-level groups.

Adding `groups=` to a field that is already in a `<form>` view does NOT auto-hide the field in the view — you must also add `groups=` to the corresponding `<field name="...">` tag in the XML view, otherwise the field remains visible regardless of the Python field definition.

snippet_text:
<record id="sale_order_personal_rule" model="ir.rule">
    <field name="name">Personal Orders</field>
    <field ref="model_sale_order" name="model_id"/>
    <field name="domain_force">['|',('user_id','=',user.id),('user_id','=',False)]</field>
    <field name="groups" eval="[(4, ref('sales_team.group_sale_salesman'))]"/>
</record>
gotchas: The `'|'` prefix operator applies to the NEXT TWO conditions only — `['|', A, B, C]` means `(A OR B) AND C`, NOT `A OR B OR C`; for a 3-way OR write `['|', '|', A, B, C]`.

In `domain_force`, `user` is a special ORM-evaluated variable bound to the current user's `res.users` record — it is NOT available in Python `search([...])` API calls; use `self.env.user.id` there instead.
Mixing `'|'` and `'&'` prefixes without careful counting causes silent logic errors — draw the expression tree first; Odoo's domain normaliser will not warn about unbalanced prefix operators.

snippet_text:
<field name="tax_id"
       widget="many2many_tags"
       domain="[('type_tax_use', '=', 'sale'),
                ('company_id', 'parent_of', parent.company_id),
                ('country_id', '=', parent.tax_country_id)]"/>
gotchas: `parent_of` returns all ancestors of the given record including itself; `child_of` returns all descendants including itself — both traverse the `parent_id` hierarchy field, which must exist on the model or `ValueError` is raised.

`parent_of` and `child_of` are evaluated in Python-side domain normalisation BEFORE sending to the database — they expand to an `in` clause with all matching IDs; on very large hierarchies (>10k nodes) this can produce queries with thousands of IDs in an `IN (...)` clause.

In view domains, `parent.company_id` refers to the PARENT FORM's `company_id` field — this syntax is only valid in view XML `domain=` attributes; using it in `model.search([...])` Python code raises `KeyError` because there is no `parent` binding in Python ORM context.

snippet_text:
<field name="name" string="Order"
    filter_domain="['|', '|',
        ('name', 'ilike', self),
        ('client_order_ref', 'ilike', self),
        ('partner_id', 'child_of', self)]"/>
<field name="partner_id" operator="child_of"/>
gotchas: `self` in `filter_domain` is the search string typed by the user (a Python string) — it is only available in search view `filter_domain` attributes; inside `ir.rule` `domain_force` or Python `search()` calls, `self` is the recordset and will not produce the expected string substitution.

`operator="child_of"` on a `<field>` in a search view changes the operator for the default search — without it, `partner_id` uses `=` (exact ID match) not hierarchy traversal; adding `operator="child_of"` enables searching all sub-partners.

Combining multiple `'|'` prefixes with `child_of` can be slow — `child_of` expands to a large `IN (...)` clause first, then the `OR` with `ilike` forces a full-table scan; index the name fields and consider limiting `child_of` to known hierarchy depths.

`message_post(body=...)` accepts HTML — do NOT pass user-controlled input directly as `body` without escaping; always wrap dynamic values in `markupsafe.Markup(...)` or use `odoo.tools.html_escape()` to prevent stored XSS in the chatter.

`_inherit = ['mail.thread']` alone adds chatter support but does NOT enable email notifications for followers — you also need `mail.thread` tracking via `tracking=True` on fields or explicit `message_subscribe()` calls; missing `_mail_post_access` can silently block chatter posts for non-admin users.

`message_post` called on a recordset with multiple records creates one message per record — if you intend to post a single message to multiple records, use `self.filtered(...)` first or iterate explicitly; broadcasting to a large recordset triggers N chatter entries.

snippet_text:
order.activity_unlink(['sale.mail_act_sale_upsell'])
for order in self:
    order_ref  = order._get_html_link()
    customer_ref = order.partner_id._get_html_link()
    order.activity_schedule(
        'sale.mail_act_sale_upsell',
        user_id=order.user_id.id or order.partner_id.user_id.id,
        note=_('Upsell %(order)s for customer %(customer)s',
               order=order_ref, customer=customer_ref)
    )
gotchas: `activity_schedule` requires `_inherit = ['mail.activity.mixin']` (in addition to `mail.thread`) — calling it on a model that only inherits `mail.thread` raises `AttributeError: 'activity_schedule'`.

Always call `activity_unlink([xml_id])` before `activity_schedule` for the same activity type to avoid duplicating activities on repeated trigger events; a missing unlink causes N activities to pile up after N button clicks.

`user_id` in `activity_schedule` must be an internal user ID — passing a portal or public user ID raises an error because activities can only be assigned to employees with a valid `employee_id` or at minimum `share=False`.

`@api.returns('mail.message', ...)` is required when overriding `message_post` — it tells the ORM that the return value is a `mail.message` record (or its ID via the lambda); omitting it breaks RPC callers that expect a message ID.
Always pass `tracking_disable=True` in context when doing silent `write()` inside `message_post` to prevent an infinite loop: `write` triggers `_track_changes` which calls `message_post` again.

`self.with_context(**so_ctx).message_post(...)` passes the modified context to super — NOT `super(...).message_post(self.with_context(...), ...)` (wrong signature); the context must be applied to `self` before the super call.

snippet_text:
<template id="report_saleorder_raw">
    <t t-call="web.html_container">
        <t t-foreach="docs" t-as="doc">
            <t t-call="sale.report_saleorder_document"
               t-lang="doc.partner_id.lang"/>
        </t>
    </t>
</template>
gotchas: `docs` is the recordset passed by `ir.actions.report` — it is available ONLY when the template is called from a report action; if you call `t-call="my_module.report_template"` directly from another template without providing `docs`, the QWeb engine raises `KeyError: docs`.

`t-lang="doc.partner_id.lang"` switches the translation context PER-DOCUMENT inside the loop — essential for multi-language batch reports; omitting it renders ALL documents in the server's default language regardless of partner preference.

The `t-as` variable (`doc`) is block-scoped to the `t-foreach` iteration; accessing `doc` outside the `t-foreach` block raises `NameError`; use `t-set` before the loop to keep a reference across blocks.

`t-set`/`t-value` reassignment inside a `t-foreach` loop accumulates across iterations only if the variable is in the SAME template scope — if the loop body calls `t-call` to a sub-template, the sub-template gets its own scope and cannot mutate the parent's `current_subtotal`.

`t-field` renders the value using the field's widget (with formatting, currency symbol, etc.) — do NOT use `t-out` for monetary fields unless you explicitly format the value; `t-out` renders the raw Python float without currency symbol.

Computed QWeb expressions in `t-value` are evaluated as Python — `line.price_subtotal` accesses the ORM field; accessing a non-existent field silently returns `0.0` (not an error) which can hide data model mismatches in report templates.

`_compute_can_publish` must be overridden in the concrete model — the base implementation grants `can_publish=True` only when the user has `write` access; if your model has custom publish permissions (e.g. only website admin), override this method or the Publish button will be incorrectly hidden.

`website_published` is a `related` field to `is_published` — setting `is_published=True` directly works and is equivalent; but `copy=False` means duplicated records start as unpublished regardless of the original, which is intentional to prevent accidental publishing of copied drafts.

`website.published.mixin` vs `website.published.multi.mixin`: the multi variant adds a `website_id` field for multi-website setups and restricts publication per-website — using the single-site mixin in a multi-website Odoo installation publishes the record on ALL websites simultaneously.
